other_observers: none; A first year Mew Gull was seen by Tony Leukering on the Pueblo Reservoir CBC on 12/17/2005, which this may be the same bird.
description: A small gull, smaller than the Ring-billed Gulls, with thinner bill. Darker mantle, and more brown on the head, chin, and throat.Behaviors: Sitting on the southeastern shore of Pueblo Reservoir with many other gulls, including Ring-billed, California, Herring, Lesser Black-backed, and Glaucous.Call: nonePlumage: first year
